-content=llama-quant : default ftype param Q5_1 --> Q8_0 ( #20828 ) Change the default ftype in llama_model_quantize_params from LLAMA_FTYPE_MOSTLY_Q5_1 to LLAMA_FTYPE_MOSTLY_Q8_0 . In case some external program naively uses the default quantization params, we should probably default to a known-good type like Q8_0 rather than Q5_1, which is rather old. macOS/iOS: macOS Apple Silicon (arm64) macOS Apple Silicon (arm64, KleidiAI enabled) macOS Intel (x64) iOS XCFramework Linux: Ubuntu x64 (CPU) Ubuntu arm64 (CPU) Ubuntu s390x (CPU) Ubuntu x64 (Vulkan) Ubuntu arm64 (Vulkan) Ubuntu x64 (ROCm 7.2) Ubuntu x64 (OpenVINO) Ubuntu x64 (SYCL FP32) Ubuntu x64 (SYCL FP16) Android: Android arm64 (CPU) Windows: Windows x64 (CPU) Windows arm64 (CPU) Windows x64 (CUDA 12) - CUDA 12.4 DLLs Windows x64 (CUDA 13) - CUDA 13.1 DLLs Windows x64 (Vulkan) Windows x64 (SYCL) Windows x64 (HIP) openEuler: openEuler x86 (310p) openEuler x86 (910b, ACL Graph) openEuler aarch64 (310p) openEuler aarch64 (910b, ACL Graph)

items.headline_en: Every Gradio Space now auto-serves an /agents
items.summary_en: Hugging Face changelog updated. Latest entry: "Every Gradio Space now auto-serves an /agents.".
items.evidence_excerpt: Changelog Keep track of latest changes on the Hugging Face Hub Apr 17, 26 Upvote 150 +145 Apr 17, 26 Spaces agents.md for your coding agents Upvote 150 +145 Every Gradio Space now auto-serves an /agents.md endpoint, a machine-readable API description that AI agents can read and call directly. Point your coding agents (like Claude Code, Codex, or Pi) at it and they figure out how to use the Space without any setup. Read the documentation to learn more. Apr 15, 26 Upvote 152 +147 Apr 15, 26 Introducing Kernels Upvote 152 +147 Kernels repositories provide an easy way to use kernels: precompiled, optimized for your exact hardware and PyTorch version, ready for torch.compile, and yields 1.7–2.5× speed-ups over baseline PyTorch. You can now browse and load Kernels from the Kernels Hub . Apr 1...
diff_excerpt: -Changelog Keep track of latest changes on the Hugging Face Hub Apr 17, 26 Upvote 149 +144 Apr 17, 26 Spaces agents.md for your coding agents Upvote 149 +144 Every Gradio Space now auto-serves an /agents.md endpoint, a machine-readable API description that AI agents can read and call directly. Point your coding agents (like Claude Code, Codex, or Pi) at it and they figure out how to use the Space without any setup. Read the documentation to learn more. Apr 15, 26 Upvote 152 +147 Apr 15, 26 Introducing Kernels Upvote 152 +147 Kernels repositories provide an easy way to use kernels: precompiled, optimized for your exact hardware and PyTorch version, ready for torch.compile, and yields 1.7–2.5× speed-ups over baseline PyTorch. You can now browse and load Kernels from the Kernels Hub . Apr 10, 26 Upvote 132 +127 Apr 10, 26 ZeroGPU overquota Upvote 132 +127 PRO users can now continue using ZeroGPU Spaces above their daily included quota. Over-quota usage requires purchasing pre-paid credits. The price is $1 per 10 minutes of over-quota ZeroGPU usage. Apr 7, 26 Upvote 117 +112 Apr 7, 26 Agent Traces on the Hub Upvote 117 +112 You can now upload traces from your agents ( Claude Code , Codex , Pi ) directly to Hugging Face Datasets. The Hub auto-detects trace formats and tags your dataset as Traces , with a dedicated viewer for browsing sessions, turns, tool calls, and model responses. No preprocessing needed, just upload the JSONL files from your local session directories as-is: Agent Local session directory Claude Code ~/.claude/projects Codex ~/.codex/sessions Pi ~/.pi/agent/sessions Useful for sharing debugging workflows, benchmarking agent behavior across models, or building training data from real coding sessions. Mar 31, 26 Upvote 129 +124 Mar 31, 26 Storage Buckets for Spaces Upvote 129 +124 You can now mount HF Buckets as persistent storage volumes directly in your Spaces . In Space settings, the new "Storage Buckets" section lets you create or select a bucket, set the mount path and access mode. You can also attach a bucket when creating a new Space. Useful for caching model weights, storing user uploads, or sharing files between Spaces under the same organization. Previous 1 2 3 ... 10 Next

+Changelog Keep track of latest changes on the Hugging Face Hub Apr 17, 26 Upvote 150 +145 Apr 17, 26 Spaces agents.md for your coding agents Upvote 150 +145 Every Gradio Space now auto-serves an /agents.md endpoint, a machine-readable API description that AI agents can read and call directly. Point your coding agents (like Claude Code, Codex, or Pi) at it and they figure out how to use the Space without any setup. Read the documentation to learn more. You can now browse and load Kernels from the Kernels Hub . Over-quota usage requires purchasing pre-paid credits. The price is $1 per 10 minutes of over-quota ZeroGPU usage. Apr 7, 26 Upvote 117 +112 Apr 7, 26 Agent Traces on the Hub Upvote 117 +112 You can now upload traces from your agents ( Claude Code , Codex , Pi ) directly to Hugging Face Datasets. The Hub auto-detects trace formats and tags your dataset as Traces , with a dedicated viewer for browsing sessions, turns, tool calls, and model responses. No preprocessing needed, just upload the JSONL files from your local session directories as-is: Agent Local session directory Claude Code ~/.claude/projects Codex ~/.codex/sessions Pi ~/.pi/agent/sessions Useful for sharing debugging workflows, benchmarking agent behavior across models, or building training data from real coding sessions.
